heading: Gray vs Gulung Mardrulk — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gray, Northern Territory and Gulung Mardrulk, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gray has a lower overall climate risk score (24/100) compared to Gulung Mardrulk (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gray or Gulung Mardrulk safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gray has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Gulung Mardrulk scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gray scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Gulung Mardrulk scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gray or Gulung Mardrulk?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gray has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
